review_notes: The functions are generally well-mapped to Kubernetes security controls related to secure authentication. However, the function 'k8s_rbac_oidc_authentication_configured' could be improved for clarity and consistency with naming conventions.
naming_improvements: Consider renaming 'k8s_rbac_oidc_authentication_configured' to 'k8s_apiserver_oidc_authentication_configured' for better alignment with the resource being configured (API server) and to maintain consistency with other API server-related checks.
title: Secure Authentication
step1_reasoning: The control 'Secure Authentication' focuses on ensuring secure authentication mechanisms are in place. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring that the API server has authentication enabled and uses RBAC for authorization. Additionally, configuring OIDC for authentication aligns with MFA requirements in cloud environments. Disabling automatic mounting of service account tokens in pods also enhances authentication security by reducing unnecessary token exposure.
